reviews.review_description:
Such a cool concept, your basically an actor who plays in various movies shooting down enemies with different weapons doging bullets, stones, lasers and tonnes more.

It is quite hard and tiring. Very tiring. Some scenes want to make me scream, others want me to enjoy the moment and feel like im there

One thing I dont like. 

Say its a very hard act and you finish that one scene thats been annoying the crap out of you and took a tonne of tries to beat it then you loose to the next scene then have to do the act all over again?

Should be at the start of the scene

Anyway would recommend

Instead of writing a review Im gonna reply to Randomgamer42 who says there is no way to recenter (not trying to make you feel dumb, just helping) you can push the right stick down (I'm saying the name so they know, do not give them hate for that) one last thing, devs, maybe put a recenter feature board showing how to in-game.

Its good fun and similar to Superhot but it is very frustrating when you have to restart from the beginning of the act everytime you get hit. Stopped playing for this reason. Hopefully they will update it so can choose to carry on from the level you were on rather than starting again from the start.

Graphics and physics are great and can be fun if it wasn't so difficult!

I played into a couple levels to try this game out and actually bought it thinking it would be fun....it is until you have lost your placement. Each time the level restarts, if your not in that exact placement then the game will move you or even turn your character. As soon as I tried to dodge something and got hit, it just made me get closer to the wall. Then after the placement has moved or turned my character, you cannot recalibrate or reset your position. I have to quit the game completely just to do it all over again. Very frustrating, overall could be good, but I've banged myself up enough to say I'm good.

So first off the game is fun like really make you smile having a great time fun, second it's a good warm up before the gym gets ya movin lol OK game play is like super hot except a bit more fun and the director just adds to the fun. Really good game

It has similar gameplay to Super Hot but it's so much better. There is more variety in levels, graphic is decent and it's pretty addictive too. Some scenes may drive you crazy but overall the game is easier than Super Hot. Highly recommended.
If your more of a rage quitter, do NOT play this. It’s a hard game. It’s fun though. It’s good action, cool concept, a little buggy and you need to be patient, but still I reccomend.
Great time moves as you move game, very good job with level design and crafted situations, pretty challenging, which I like.

Great game, real fun, nice graphics
..I enjoyed it
I LOVE THIS GAME ESPECIALLY THE SLOW MO THIS IS A CHEFS KISS WORTH I LOVE IT however~it caused my legs extreme pain from crouching to fast and to much and pls add more levels

I was thrilled to find this. The game play is similar to Superhot, but much better. The replay value is high. Trying to score higher and find the collectibles. Started with the demo and had to play more..Hopefully you enjoy as much as I do!

Really great game :) I like the game very much. The reason for giving only 4 stars is that it is not really possible to center the view as in any other game with long press on the meta-key on the controller and that I have to restart the game, when I leave the headset mid game and want to go on after a while. The start at the beginning of the scenes is ok for me. But maybe I am used to this, because I tried to beat Superhot in survival mode many months.

Overall good game it’s just a bit to challenging for me.The dodging  ￼was a nice feature but I did end up banging my head multiple times. I’d love a feature where you don’t have to restart an act completely when you die it frustrated me a little having to do one part over and over and over again. I like the little help feature where if you’re stuck it gives a gun with inf ammo though it gave it to me on the wrong part I was struggling with
Awesome
Got this on special for $8 (AUS) and it is so much fun! Very similar to Superhot which is a great game, feels like an upgrade. Highly recommend 😀

Action Hero on Quest 3 is an outstanding VR experience that perfectly captures the feeling of being inside a fast-paced action movie. Every level feels like a carefully directed set piece, combining slow-motion mechanics with intense, cinematic moments that make you feel like the star of your own blockbuster.
The gameplay is intuitive, satisfying, and incredibly immersive. Dodging bullets, grabbing weapons mid-air, and reacting in slow motion creates a constant sense of flow and excitement. The visual presentation and level design are top-tier for VR, and the game runs smoothly on Quest 3.
Each “film scenario” offers a distinct vibe, keeping the experience fresh and engaging throughout. It’s short, but highly replayable thanks to the arcade-style structure and creative scenarios.

Developer suggestion:
One feature that would elevate the experience even further would be the option to replay completed scenes in normal real-time speed after finishing them. Watching your own actions unfold without slow motion would add a whole new layer of satisfaction, allowing players to appreciate their performance like watching a real action movie playback. It would make the experience feel even more cinematic and memorable.
Overall, Action Hero is a must-play for VR fans who want intense, stylish action with a strong arcade feel.

I loved this game, it’s even better then Super Hot in every way accept that it doesn’t have the endless mode that was my favorite part of super hot.  The graphics are really nicely done (it looks way better than super hot), and the levels are really creative.  I loved a lot of the levels—the car chase scene, the gravity changing hallway scene, the elevator ride scene, the Aztec boss scene.  In truth I haven’t finished the last two movies yet, but the game is very fun and very well made and I’m looking forward to playing them.  I would have liked the game even more with longer scenarios in which you progressed seamlessly through the different movies and/or with an endless mode where you see how high of a score you can get before getting overwhelmed, but I don’t regret this purchase at all.  Heck, release longer scenarios or an endless mode as DLC and I’ll buy them.  Not an immersive RPG or shooter, but a really, really fun game that had me moving a lot (but slowly) in the real world.  Easily recommended.

Great Game!

Agree with the other positive reviews…..

If you move slow then this is not for you 😂😂

How about an Action Hero 2?


Currently have no issues!

How about adding to action hero a portal or movie set door way that you walk thru to start the game in Mixed Reality?

One error and you have to do the whole scenario again 
And not just that scene, not worth full price unfortunately.

I love the game, after playing superhot it is so FUN. But there are some gimmicks, and there isn’t a reset view option when you hold the quest button. Which caused me to break a vase about 10 minutes ago. Was the vase completely my fault, yes. Could it have been prevented by a reset view option, yes. Please add one.

Basically super hot but way more fun. Levels have themes and replay ability is good. Lots of levels, in themes of movies, like a matrix movie and a dinosaur one where u fight them on a train. It’s peak

reviews.review_title: Superhot has got nothing on this.
reviews.review_description:
You took what made Superhot great & crafted it into a fun story & offers weekly challenges. I love almost everything about it. The option to fast forward where you start twirling your gun like a bada$$ for a higher score.

How it allows you to tweak the settings giving you more ammo & all that. (Helpful when needing to practice a level you just can't seem to get past.)


The stakes feel higher when you're on a moving train/semi, with dinosaurs & dirt bikes flying at you. There are also ninjas with swords & throwing stars appearing out of nowhere to attack. It's essentially Superhot on steroids, & is just a really fun (yet sometimes frustrating having to restart the whole level when you fail at any act) game.

My one & only hangup is that all the final bosses I've versed so far (Act 4's) have been so incredibly easy. A final boss should really be some type of a challenge. Not to simply grab a rocket launcher & fire it once straight ahead. That was kind of lame.

With that said I'd have to give it a 4.7/5.

Edit: After playing some more I have another issue with not being able to recenter. No idea why it doesn't seem to work like other games where you just have to hold the meta button for a couple seconds. With this game I find myself butted up against my boundary with no way to fix it. 

The fact this issue has been noted by many reviewers yet still not addressed takes my almost perfect rating down to a 3\5. Smh
Funp
Best game EVER

I think this game is awesome and the action movie aesthetic's put it on another level. In my opinion, this game is way better than Superhot. The only recommendation I could give would be to add save points in the movie chapters so that we don't always have to start from the beginning of the movie. Sometimes I have to jump to another game for commitments and I lose progress. Or add a chapter select screen to each movie so that we can manually choose a chapter once we've already completed it. Adding something like that would make it really awesome. I highly recommend this game!

I'm gonna be really honest here. This game is a ripoff of superhot, and I'm gonna mention superhot here a lot.
Sure, the graphics are nice, the gameplay is good, since they ripoff superhot.
But what's wrong with it then? 
Let me tell you.
In superhot, when you die, you die because YOU made a mistake. You learn from your mistake and you'll progress.
In this clone, when you die, you die because a very bad level design, you might die because the game don't realy understand that you have moved out of the way of a trap or bullets.
I died numerous times from bullets even when standing beside them, but the game thinks I got hit.
And when you die, there is NO checkpoint on that certain act, so you have to start over from the beginning every time!.
This is tedious as $hit and it just breaks the mood of wanna play this.
I really can't understand why people give this game 5/5 star.

Do yourself a favor, skip this clone and go for the original, it is just so much better in every way!
Go get Superhot instead!
had fun with it.  good price.  And you can adjust difficulty level if you are getting frustrated with a scene.

reviews.review_title: Not perfect but its what you're looking
reviews.review_description: Great stuff

For the devs eyes only: The game itself, the movies, and the art style is great, but I have a couple recommendations. 

First of all, checkpoints. If you look at a similar game like super hot, they have only around 3 scenarios per checkpoint, but your game is split into around five scenes. This can make gameplay much less engaging because you constantly have to go through three or four scenes you already played for a retry on one later scene you can’t beat. If you could shorten the checkpoints to every three or four scenes, it would make gameplay much more engaging. 

Secondly, timing. You don’t really want to throw enemies and streams of bullets at the player immediately when they enter a scene, especially if it’s not the start of a scene and they switch from a previous scene straight into a scene with multiple dangers flying at them with no time to react. It would be much better if you gave the player about 1.5 seconds to register the scene before enemies start shooting and enter the scene.this makes it so the player spends more time dying to actual fights, and not getting shot immediately when entering a new scene because they can’t see the bullet that was shot at them immediately as the scene started flying at them.

I would appreciate it if you respond as soon as possible, though I know you are taking a break from updates due to “unfortunate circumstances “ and obstacles. Hope you overcome these.

Its one of my all time favorite games. Just buy it.  It's Not too easy, not too hard, plus adjustments in settings to customize difficulty levels in different ways. It's got that bullet time/ superhot "chrono-tempo" (time moves when you move) locomotion and many different types of weapons (katana sword,  ninja stars, throwing knives, Machine guns, pistols, shot guns, grenades, wrenches, lead pipes, your fists, and of course THE GOLDEN GUN![unlimited ammo].  I beat the game and now I am working on " directors cut". Which means you can play the game twice all the way through and its different the second time. Plus some regular add movies when updates are added.

A very cool re-imagination of Superhot. It really makes you feel like you're inside a movie. The only downside for me: it's more of a memory game than a reaction-based one. You have to memorize each scene over and over until it becomes automatic. By the end, this process gets tedious and boring.

This game plays very close to SUPER HOT, but in a more colorful and fun way. SUPER HOT was fun, but most of the levels were pretty similar. This game is way more colorful, and each level has its own twists. Some can be pretty challenging but makes it all the more re-wording when you complete it. Not sure if the devs will add more chapters in the future but would be really cool if they did. If you enjoyed Super hot, you should enjoy this a well. If you did not enjoy Super Hot, there is still a chance you would enjoy this, as it's similar but yet different enough to pull new people in. Would be nice if the game would recenter back to the original position so I can walk back into it.
Took a few trys to nail it but got the role

reviews.review_title: Indiana jones and superhot had a baby….
reviews.review_description: If your a fan of puzzle style FPS than Action Hero is your game. It takes all the things that made superhot awesome and cranks it up! Not blowing smoke… this game feels like Indiana Jones and superhot had a baby while Micheal Bay directed to process. The scenes are fun and intense. I have a brief review on YouTube. Look for me on there PR-IN-VR and check my review playlist.

Superhot once came up with a very cool concept for a first-person-shooter. Time only moves, when the player moves. This led to some interesting gameplay merging puzzler and shooter into a unique experience. Action Hero pretty much copies this idea 1:1 and puts it into a fresh setting. At a film set the player jumps into the role of a stuntman that has to do the action sequences of several movies. There are five movies plus a special feature to participate in that come with their own unique setting. Enemies and available weapons vary with the setting. For example in the martial arts movie the player fights Ninjas with shurikens and blades while in the spy movie it's more down to guns and rocket launchers. 

Each scene is like a small puzzle. Enemies and weapons appear on screen and whenever the player moves, enemies and bullets do too. The player has to physically dodge incoming damage while shooting, slashing or boxing the enemies when they are in range. Just like in Superhot this often leads to trial and error situations. But with careful planning and the right moves at the right time every situation can be mastered. The game is challenging and after beating all levels there are harder variants (director’s cut) on each scene. But there are also options to bring down the difficulty so that everybody can have fun with this.

On the technical side there isn’t anything to complain about in Action Hero. The graphics are sharp, colorful and fluid. I just found the artstyle a bit bland. The game is also light on content because all the movies are very short. The director’s cuts and weekly online challenges with leaderboards help with replayability a bit at least. All things considered, this is a good Superhot clone that lacks the unmistakable style of the original, but comes up with its own fun idea for a setting.

I played the game because I was looking for a fun shooter. Action Hero delivered just that: It's like an advanced version of Superhot, in which you go through multiple "movies" (themed levels) like a spy/agent  one, or one where you fight as a Ninja. There's some really cool references, like from the Matrix, the hallway fight from Inception or Indiana Jones. Gunplay is very good, but especially throwing things or even guns is much more accurate and smoother than in Superhot. The crotch shots are really funny and satisfying too. This game is now my new favourite shooter, and it's nice that it's quite a lot longer than Superhot.

This game is straight-up awesome. You feel like the main character in an action movie, dodging bullets in slow motion and pulling off crazy stunts. The different movie themes keep it from getting stale, and the combat is just super satisfying. Graphics aren’t mind-blowing, but who cares when the gameplay is this fun? If you’re into VR and action, this is 100% worth playing.

10/10

As the other reviews say, this is a Super Hot imitator, and a really good one. The devs took the term "action movie simulator" literally, as you play the lead actor in a series of action movies. 

Only a couple complaints so far: 
1) The guns have huge grab boxes, so if you have a gun in your right hand, and try to make a fist with your left hand, the gun will switch to your left.
2) The swords in the third movie don't feel like swords. It seems that the swords don't register each other; instead they pass through each other as if the other sword doesn't exist. 

Other than that, it's a spectacular game that you need to play to fully appreciate.

Excellent game. A blatant copy of Superhot but done very well, done respectfully and with a cool cinematic theme. 
Recommended.
Good
I hate that everytime you die you restart the act and not just the part that you died in please make a setting for it

reviews.review_title: Perfect Blend of Badassery and Challenge
reviews.review_description: If you’ve played super hot and even remotely enjoyed it, you WILL love this game. It flawlessly combines the incredible “bullet-time” gameplay formula with just the right amount of humor and whimsy that reminds me of I Expect You to Die (also a very good game). Makes you feel like a total bad*** while at the same time providing a pretty substantial challenge if you want to grind for gold or even platinum. Of course, if you’re not into that, you can also just dodge bullets and enjoy the explosions . Finally, to whoever voice acted the director (both male and female), you did an incredible job. The character really makes the game for me.

reviews.review_title: A very substandard copy of superhot
reviews.review_description: The graphics are not bad but the game mechanics are so poorly implemented its not worth finishing

Play through each scene in slow motion and then go, I thought it would not be challenging enough but I was wrong, it had me hooked from the start, each scene could be played in different ways which adds to the entertainment and with the developers promising more to come I think this will be a winner.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review, more movies are indeed in production!

reviews.review_description: Just played through the first "movie" and this is 100% a sequel to SuperHot, the game play mechanics are identical but the graphics are much better and each "room" is a scene from a movie you are filming which gives a level of context not present in SuperHot (which even in 2025 is still a top 10 vr game in my opinion). So for anyone who loved SuperHot or maybe never gave it a chance because the graphics looked so simplistic, I say buy Action Hero, it's very hard for me to imagine anyone not enjoying this, it still amazes me that we don't see this time mechanic used more often in vr, these two games manage to combine a fps with puzzle solving as the order to you do things can make the difference between success and failure, they are a unique expirence in vr, so again just buy it.

Great game, it's basically a rethemed Superhot with better graphics and a few additions to make it more engaging.
I have one point of feedback. The boundary positioning, from scene to scene, is awkward.  Say I walk to the edge of my boundary, the next scene starts there, so I can't move forward or backwards. You can reset the position by pressing the joystick, but this interrupts the game's flow. With the movie theme, it might be simple to just have a quick "take your mark" and highlight the starting point to move to when transitioning scenes. But I'm sure there are better ways to solve it as well.

reviews.review_title: Good SUPERHOT clone, but very repetative
reviews.review_description:
The game is based off of action movie tropes with a movie director 'directing' you on each scene.

The game is pretty fun, and it's very active, but the game play it's basically memorizing every room, and some rooms the enemies immediately shoot before you can even grab a weapon so you'll be dying a lot. The rag-doll physics and enemy deaths are pretty cool, especially in slow motion.

There's hardly any checkpoints after each 'room'
sometimes you have to get past 5 or 6 rooms (scenes) before it saves, and if you decide to save & quit from the pause menu, it will show the current act then gives a warning that if you exit it 'deletes temporary progress and cannot be undone'.  So what does it save?    It then starts you back at the first Act of the movie if you select it again from the main menu.

And there's also seems to be a bug where it resets the center of your play space when you die and spawns your position how you were at the time (crouching, or ducking to the side) and seems like that will make it your center.  One level in the elevators I kept dying on and after several tries it had moved my play space off to the side, and even pressing in the thumb stick and holding the question button to reset didn't recenter the center of the elevator, so I had to quit the game.

Store says Supported player modes: Roomscale, Standing
But in the options there doesn't seem to be a way to select between standing or room scale.

I love this game the only thing they could improve would be adding dual gripping to increase aim and make us feel like a true Action hero 
It doesn’t have to be on every weapons but the smgs and large melee weapons would be cool
￼ it’s just so breathtaking and the fact of the matter is it’s just makes you feel so free walking all those characters and shooting them is just a very fun experience
